LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TANYA MURILLO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO ALLOW A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE TO REMOVE THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING AND TO MAKE OTHER MINOR CHANGES AS REFLECTED IN THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT FILED ON JULY 20, 2007. THE REQUEST TO AMEND TO ADD A 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUANTUM MERIT IS DENIED FOR IT WAS NOT TIMELY FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JEANNINE CLARK AND JAQUELINE BADENHAUSEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOCUSES ONLY ON WHETHER THE JANUARY 3, 2006 WRITING CONSTITUTES AN ENFORCEABLE WRITTEN AGREEMENT. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ENFORCE BOTH A WRITTEN AND AN ORAL AGREEMENT THAT IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF PAST PERFORMANCE, THE ORAL AGREEMENT MAY BE ENFORCEABLE. (1 MILLER & STARR, CAL. REAL ESTATE 2D (1989) SECTION 1:60 P. 168) THE MOVING PAPERS DID NOT ADDRESS THIS THEORY OF RECOVERY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-28-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JEANNINE CLARK AND JAQUELINE BADENHAUSEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: PHIL HORNE, ESQUIRE (415.577.1170) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOCUSES ONLY ON WHETHER THE JANUARY 3, 2006 WRITING CONSTITUTES AN ENFORCEABLE WRITTEN AGREEMENT. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ENFORCE BOTH A WRITTEN AND AN ORAL AGREEMENT THAT IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF PAST PERFORMANCE, THE ORAL AGREEMENT MAY BE ENFORCEABLE. (1 MILLER & STARR, CAL. REAL ESTATE 2D (1989) SECTION 1:60 P. 168) THE MOVING PAPERS DID NOT ADDRESS THIS THEORY OF RECOVERY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF TANYA MURILLO'S MOTION TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: PHILIP S. HORNE, ESQUIRE (415.552.2740) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES M. MILLAR, ESQUIRE (MILLAR & ASSOCIATES, 415.981.8100) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED, WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENTS IS CONTROLLING. ADDING ALCALA WILL NOT PREJUDICE DEFENDANTS FOR THEY WERE THE ONES TO RAISE THE ISSUE BY ASSERTING THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. Off Calendar Jun-20-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Philip S. Horne, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Tanya Murillo
James M. Millar, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Jeannine E. Clark

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Tania Murillo's motion to compel attendance at deposition is granted. Counsel for defendant is ordered to contact counsel for plaintiff to re-schedule the depositions at a mutually convenient time. The depositions shall be completed within 21 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $852.25 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
